summary:
Claim: U in the urea clearance formula represents urinary concentration in mg/mL.  
Evidence: (1) Dimensional analysis shows combining volume-based variables (U and V) results in invalid units (mL²/min), ruling out urine volume per minute (D). (2) Clinical standards define U as urinary concentration—such as urea at mg/mL—in GFR and clearance equations.  
Answer: B
formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 1 summarized): Claim: U in the urea clearance formula represents urinary concentration in mg/mL.  
Evidence: (1) Dimensional analysis shows combining volume-based variables (U and V) results in invalid units (mL²/min), ruling out urine volume per minute (D). (2) Clinical standards define U as urinary concentration—such as urea at mg/mL—in GFR and clearance equations.  
Answer: B. Answer choice: B
